content_preview.title: Florida Calls a Special Session on AI as DeSantis Defies Trump and His Own Party to Regulate Chatbots for Children
content_preview.summary: DeSantis is forcing a second vote on his AI Bill of Rights in a special session opening April 28, despite White House opposition and a criminal probe into OpenAI over the FSU shooting.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Florida Governor Ron DeSantis is forcing a fourth-quarter gamble on artificial intelligence regulation, adding his stalled "AI Bill of Rights" to the agenda of a special legislative session set to open April 28 in Tallahassee. The move puts DeSantis in direct confrontation with President Trump and Florida's own House Speaker — an unusual alliance between a Republican governor and federal AI deregulators — and comes days after Florida's Attorney General announced a criminal investiga...

findings_appended.message: 'Six others wounded' not confirmed by accessible sources — CBS says 'several'; charge counts suggest 7
findings_appended.details: The article states the FSU shooting 'killed two people and wounded six others.' CBS News (fetched) says only 'Two people were killed and several others wounded' — not six. NBC News (403 blocked) is cited alongside CBS, but the specific number cannot be verified from accessible sources. Additionally, Ikner faces 'seven counts of attempted first-degree murder' (confirmed by CBS), which implies 7 non-fatal victims, not 6. The 'six' figure is unconfirmed and potentially contradicted by the charge count. Fix: change 'six others' to 'several others' (matching CBS), or independently verify the specific number and cite accordingly.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-reported 1,184-word human-requested News piece on a major AI regulation flashpoint. The Florida/DeSantis angle is genuinely distinctive — Republican governor vs. Republican president on AI regulation. The FSU criminal investigation provides strong news hook. 'What We Don't Know' is strong on Section 230 obstacles.
editor_notes.source_verification: CBS News (FSU shooting): fetched — AG Uthmeier 'if it was a person on the other end' quote ✓; ChatGPT queries (weapon selection, peak times, campus location) ✓; Ikner two first-degree murder + seven attempted murder ✓; October trial ✓; OpenAI denial quote ✓; subpoena details ✓; April 21 announcement ✓. BUT: CBS says 'several others wounded', NOT 'six others wounded' — this is an unresolved discrepancy ✗. CBS News (DOJ task force): fetched — January 9, 2026 announcement ✓; Pam Bondi leading ✓; David Sacks consulting ✓; three legal grounds ✓. White House EO: fetched — December 11, 2025 ✓; BEAD Program broadband conditioning explicitly confirmed in Section 5(a) ✓ (the Round 1 $42B figure was correctly removed; the broadband conditioning claim itself is now properly attributed to the actual EO). The Next Web: fetched — 1,208 AI bills in all 50 states in 2025 ✓; 145 enacted ✓; 78 chatbot safety bills in 27 states ✓; Senate 99-1 vote ✓; Commerce Dept flagging Colorado/California/New York in March 2026 ✓. Axios: returned 403 on manual fetch (automated check confirms accessible via sources_reachable: true). NBC News: returned 403 on manual fetch.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Both Round 1 errors corrected: (1) $42B broadband claim removed — replaced with accurate description of the actual EO provision, now attributed to whitehouse.gov which confirms the broadband funding conditioning ✓; (2) 'wealthiest' direct quote removed — replaced with accurate paraphrase 'enormous economic and political power' ✓. Remaining issue: 'six others wounded' is attributed to both CBS and NBC, but CBS only says 'several.' Ikner's seven attempted murder charges suggest 7 wounded, not 6. The specific 'six' figure needs either confirmation or correction to 'several.'
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Balanced and neutral on a politically charged topic. Appropriately presents the DeSantis vs. Trump dynamic as factual without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: Human-requested article on Florida AI special session. No prior Herald coverage of this specific state-level development.
concerns: 'Six others wounded' not confirmed by CBS (says 'several') and potentially contradicted by seven attempted murder charge counts
recommendations: Change 'wounded six others' to 'wounded several others' to match CBS reporting, or independently verify the specific number from NBC or another accessible source
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 re-review. Both Round 1 errors correctly resolved. One remaining issue: the 'six wounded' casualty count is not confirmed by accessible sources and may be incorrect. Single targeted fix needed.
